methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pipedream operates in the Transportation sector and reached an acquired exit after raising $22.4M in total capital. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 private peers in Transportation (Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft), with no public comparables identified.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. A focused comparable company approach requiring same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than three datapoints within those filters, falling short of the minimum threshold needed for reliable triangulation. While 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, none satisfied the narrow stage and tier constraints necessary to anchor a defensible multiple-based valuation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive dispersion and misrepresent the true comp set.

Given the constraints of the available data, this analysis cannot support a quantified valuation range through traditional triangulation methods. Any conclusion would require either expansion of the peer set to include adjacent sectors, incorporation of transaction multiples from prior fundraising rounds, or disclosure of deal-specific terms from the acquisition itself. The fundamental limitation is the sparse intersection of Transportation-specific peers at comparable stages and scale, which undermines confidence in multiple-based synthesis.
